meta_title: For Founders: content creation to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to content creation and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Content Creation to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with no project ideas. Learn how to use content creation as a discovery engine for SaaS. A professional case study using Antigravity and Claude.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for the 'perfect' idea. Instead, use content creation to document your learning and build in public. By sharing technical insights, you attract an audience that reveals their pain points. Use Antigravity to scaffold prototypes from those insights and Claude Code to iterate at high velocity.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the silent killer of ambition. You have the technical stack, the ambition, and the hunger to ship, yet you are paralyzed by the absence of a viable project. This isn't a failure of capability; it is a failure of feedback loops.

Great SaaS products are rarely invented in a vacuum; they are discovered through the friction of your own content. By transforming your technical journey into a content engine, you stop guessing what the market wants and start observing where the audience hits a wall. This is how you pivot from a builder without a cause to a founder with a waitlist.

steps.title: Define Your Niche Authority
steps.description: Identify a technical domain where you have intermediate competence. We aren't looking for a product yet; we are looking for a community to serve.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a market researcher. Analyze the top 20 trending repositories in [Domain] and identify three recurring pain points that developers discuss in issues but lack a SaaS solution for.
steps.claude_code_command: claude research --topic 'recurring developer pain points in [Domain]'

steps.title: Build a Content Discovery Loop
steps.description: Create a micro-tool that solves one small piece of the pain point identified in step 1. Publish the code and the explanation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, modular TypeScript starter for a CLI tool that automates [Pain Point]. Include a README that frames this as an experimental solution.

steps.title: Analyze Engagement Data
steps.description: Observe which parts of your content receive the most questions. These questions are your future product roadmap.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the sentiment and questions from my recent post on [Topic]. Categorize the top 3 requests for additional functionality.

steps.title: Rapid MVP Prototyping
steps.description: Use the feedback to build a high-value MVP component. Focus on speed-to-market over feature completeness.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Convert the CLI tool into a Next.js API endpoint that exposes the [Feature] functionality. Ensure robust error handling and production-ready structure.

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Ship the MVP to your audience. The goal is to get the first 10 users to sign up for a waitlist or provide feedback.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a landing page copy that highlights the 'before' and 'after' state of using my tool. Focus on the core value proposition derived from user feedback.

pro_tips: Never build for 'everyone'; build for the specific subset of developers who complained about your last tutorial.
Use Claude Code to document your code as you write it; this creates a self-sustaining content library for your future marketing.
If your content isn't getting engagement, you aren't solving a painful enough problem—pivot the content topic, not the code.
Focus on 'utility-first' marketing: your code should be a tool that solves a problem, not just a blog post that describes it.
Treat your GitHub commits as a newsletter; people follow builders who show their work in real-time.
content.next_steps: Once you have your first 10 users, prioritize the most requested feature from your feedback logs. Use Claude Code to build it in 48 hours and launch it back to your audience.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for inspiration and start building the feedback loop that dictates your product's success. Your next big idea is hidden in the comments of your last post. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
